description.en: Thailand's second-largest island, Koh Samui is a tropical paradise famous for palm-fringed beaches, luxury resorts, and the iconic Big Buddha Temple. A gateway to the Gulf islands with vibrant nightlife and pristine nature.
description.nl: Het op een na grootste eiland van Thailand, Koh Samui is een tropisch paradijs beroemd om zijn palmbomen-omzoomde stranden, luxe resorts en de iconische Grote Boeddha Tempel. Een toegangspoort tot de Golfeilanden met bruisend nachtleven en ongerepte natuur.

metaTitle.en: Discover Koh Samui | What to do in Koh Samui?
metaTitle.nl: Ontdek Koh Samui | Wat te doen op Koh Samui?

enhanced_description:
The moment the plane dips low over the Gulf of Thailand and Koh Samui appears below — a lush green jewel fringed by white sand and turquoise water — you know this island is something special. I landed at the charming open-air Samui Airport, where the terminal is essentially a collection of thatched-roof pavilions surrounded by tropical gardens, and immediately felt the pace of life shift. A songthaew rattled me along the coastal road to Chaweng, past swaying coconut palms and roadside stalls selling fresh coconut ice cream for 40 baht. The warm, salt-tinged breeze through the open windows felt like a welcome home.

Chaweng Beach is where most visitors begin, and for good reason. The three-kilometer stretch of powder-fine sand slopes gently into the Gulf, and on calm days the water is so clear you can see your feet at waist depth. I spent mornings walking the northern end of Chaweng, where the crowds thin out near Chaweng Noi, stopping at a small Thai-run coffee stand called Jungle Club viewpoint for an iced Americano with a panoramic view that felt stolen from a postcard. In the evenings, Chaweng's Soi Green Mango pulses with music from the bars, but I preferred the quieter stretch near Ark Bar, where you can sit with your toes in the sand and watch fire dancers perform against the sunset.

The spiritual heart of the island is Wat Phra Yai, known to everyone as the Big Buddha Temple. I arrived early one morning before the tour buses, climbing the wide staircase flanked by naga serpents as monks in saffron robes swept the temple grounds. The 12-meter golden Buddha sits serenely on a small islet connected to the northeast coast, and from the platform you can see across to Koh Phangan. A elderly monk gestured for me to sit, and we shared a few minutes of silence looking out over the water — no words, just presence. Down at the base, a circle of market stalls sells amulets, handmade soaps, and the best roti mataba I found on the island, stuffed with chicken curry and served by a smiling woman who told me she had been there for twenty years.

One day I hired a longtail from the fishing village of Bophut to explore Ang Thong Marine National Park. The archipelago of 42 limestone islands rising from the emerald sea is staggering — sheer cliffs draped in jungle, hidden saltwater lagoons, and beaches accessible only by kayak. We anchored at Koh Mae Ko and I hiked the steep trail to the emerald lake viewpoint, arriving breathless and drenched in sweat, but the sight of the jade-green lagoon encircled by jungle-covered karst towers was worth every step. Back on the boat, the captain cracked open a cooler of fresh pineapple and we ate in silence, the only sound the water lapping against the hull.

Fisherman's Village in Bophut became my favorite evening ritual. Every Friday night the narrow street transforms into a walking market where local families set up stalls selling grilled prawns, som tam, and coconut pancakes. I befriended a vendor named Khun Noi who made extraordinary pad kra pao — holy basil stir-fry with a fiery kick that cleared my sinuses and made me order a second plate immediately. Between the market stalls, boutique shops sell handmade jewelry and Thai silk, and live bands play on small stages. The atmosphere is worlds apart from the thumping clubs of Chaweng — it is relaxed, familial, and genuinely local.

Koh Samui rewards those who venture beyond the main beaches. I rented a scooter and traced the island's ring road south to Lamai Beach, where the bizarre Hin Ta and Hin Yai rock formations jut from the shore, then continued to the quiet Lipa Noi coast where I had an entire beach to myself for an afternoon. In the hilly interior, the Na Muang waterfalls cascade into natural swimming pools surrounded by jungle, and the Secret Buddha Garden on the island's highest peak hides dozens of stone sculptures among the moss and ferns. At sunset I parked at Laem Sor Pagoda, a golden chedi on the island's southern tip, and watched the sky turn from gold to violet with nothing but the sound of cicadas for company. Koh Samui is an island that gives you exactly the experience you seek — wild or luxurious, raucous or serene — and always, always beautiful.

description.en: Hidden in the jungle-covered hills of Samui's interior, this mystical garden features dozens of stone statues and figures scattered among moss-covered rocks and towering trees. Built by a local farmer in 1976, it offers a magical escape from the beaches below.
description.nl: Verborgen in de jungle-bedekte heuvels van Samui's binnenland, heeft deze mystieke tuin tientallen stenen beelden en figuren verspreid tussen mosbegroeid gesteente en hoge bomen. Gebouwd door een lokale boer in 1976, biedt het een magische ontsnapping van de stranden beneden.

description.en: A tranquil west-coast beach largely untouched by tourism, perfect for watching spectacular sunsets over the Gulf of Thailand. Local fishing boats dot the shoreline and the shallow water stretches far out, making it ideal for families.
description.nl: Een rustig strand aan de westkust, grotendeels ongeraakt door toerisme, perfect om spectaculaire zonsondergangen boven de Golf van Thailand te bekijken. Lokale vissersboten staan langs de kustlijn en het ondiepe water strekt zich ver uit, ideaal voor families.

description.en: A peaceful forest monastery at the base of a waterfall in the island's interior. Monks meditate here among the rocks and flowing water. The short jungle trail is lined with mindfulness signs and natural beauty.
description.nl: Een vredig bosklooster aan de voet van een waterval in het binnenland van het eiland. Monniken mediteren hier tussen de rotsen en stromend water. Het korte jungle-pad is omzoomd met mindfulness-borden en natuurlijke schoonheid.

description.en: The charming old Chinese shophouses along Bophut's main street house independent cafes and galleries that most tourists overlook during non-market days. Try Karma Sutra restaurant for fusion Thai tapas with a sea view.
description.nl: De charmante oude Chinese winkelpanden langs de hoofdstraat van Bophut herbergen onafhankelijke cafes en galerieën die de meeste toeristen overslaan op niet-marktdagen. Probeer het Karma Sutra restaurant voor fusion Thaise tapas met zeezicht.

description.en: A striking golden pagoda perched on the quiet southern tip of the island, overlooking pristine coastline. Rarely visited by tourists, it offers some of the most peaceful sunset views on Samui with no crowds in sight.
description.nl: Een opvallende gouden pagode gelegen op de rustige zuidpunt van het eiland, met uitzicht over de ongerepte kustlijn. Zelden bezocht door toeristen, biedt het een van de meest vredige zonsondergangen op Samui zonder drukte in zicht.

description.en: A beloved beachfront restaurant in Fisherman's Village serving fresh seafood and authentic Thai dishes. Their grilled red snapper with tamarind sauce is legendary among locals and expats alike.
description.nl: Een geliefd strandrestaurant in Fisherman's Village dat verse zeevruchten en authentieke Thaise gerechten serveert. Hun gegrilde rode snapper met tamarindesaus is legendarisch onder zowel locals als expats.

description.en: The most authentic street food on the island, located at the southern end of Chaweng. Local vendors serve pad kra pao, som tam, and grilled chicken at unbeatable prices. The moo ping (grilled pork skewers) here are the best on Samui.
description.nl: Het meest authentieke straatvoedsel op het eiland, gelegen aan het zuidelijke uiteinde van Chaweng. Lokale verkopers serveren pad kra pao, som tam en gegrilde kip tegen onverslaanbare prijzen. De moo ping (gegrilde varkens spiesjes) hier zijn de beste van Samui.

description.en: Part of Six Senses Samui resort, this stunning cliffside restaurant offers multi-course tasting menus with panoramic ocean views. A once-in-a-lifetime dining experience with creative Thai-inspired cuisine by top chefs.
description.nl: Onderdeel van het Six Senses Samui resort, dit prachtige klifrestaurant biedt meergangen proefmenu's met panoramisch zeezicht. Een eenmalige eetervaring met creatieve Thais-geinspireerde gerechten door topkoks.

description.en: A humble stall at the Friday Night Market in Bophut serving what many consider the best pad kra pao on the island. Khun Noi uses fresh holy basil from her garden and a secret chili paste recipe passed down from her grandmother.
description.nl: Een bescheiden kraam op de vrijdagavondmarkt in Bophut die serveert wat velen beschouwen als de beste pad kra pao op het eiland. Khun Noi gebruikt verse holy basil uit haar tuin en een geheim chilipasta recept van haar grootmoeder.

description.en: A stylish Lamai Beach restaurant blending Mediterranean and Thai flavors. Known for their excellent wine list, fresh oysters, and superb duck massaman curry. Romantic atmosphere with candlelit tables and ocean breezes.
description.nl: Een stijlvol restaurant aan Lamai Beach dat mediterrane en Thaise smaken combineert. Bekend om hun uitstekende wijnkaart, verse oesters en voortreffelijke eend massaman curry. Romantische sfeer met kaarslicht en oceaanbries.

description.en: Perched on a hillside overlooking a private beach, this world-class resort features private pool villas with stunning Gulf views. Impeccable service, a renowned spa, and multiple dining options make it the island's premier luxury stay.
description.nl: Gelegen op een heuvel met uitzicht op een privéstrand, biedt dit wereldklasse resort privé zwembadhuisjes met prachtig uitzicht op de Golf. Onberispelijke service, een beroemde spa en meerdere restaurants maken het de beste luxe verblijfplek op het eiland.

description.en: A beachfront luxury resort in the heart of Fisherman's Village. Thai-inspired architecture, an infinity pool overlooking the sea, and easy walking access to Bophut's restaurants and night market.
description.nl: Een luxe strandresort in het hart van Fisherman's Village. Thais-geinspireerde architectuur, een infinity pool met zeezicht en loopafstand van Bophut's restaurants en nachtmarkt.

description.en: Well-positioned mid-range resort right on Chaweng Beach with spacious rooms, a large pool, and excellent breakfast buffet. Great value for a beachfront location on Samui's most popular stretch of sand.
description.nl: Goed gelegen middenklasse resort direct aan Chaweng Beach met ruime kamers, een groot zwembad en uitstekend ontbijtbuffet. Uitstekende waarde voor een strandlocatie op Samui's populairste strook zand.

description.en: A clean and sociable hostel near Chaweng, popular with backpackers and solo travelers. Air-conditioned dorms, a small pool, and a communal kitchen keep costs low while the location keeps you close to everything.
description.nl: Een schoon en gezellig hostel bij Chaweng, populair bij backpackers en soloreizigers. Slaapzalen met airconditioning, een klein zwembad en een gemeenschappelijke keuken houden de kosten laag terwijl de locatie je dicht bij alles houdt.

description.en: Modern and reliable hotel near Fisherman's Village with clean rooms, pool access, and solid amenities. An excellent base for exploring the north coast at a reasonable price point.
description.nl: Modern en betrouwbaar hotel bij Fisherman's Village met schone kamers, zwembadtoegang en goede voorzieningen. Een uitstekende uitvalsbasis om de noordkust te verkennen voor een redelijke prijs.

content.en: Rent a scooter (200-300 THB/day) to explore Samui's ring road at your own pace. Songthaews (shared pickup trucks) run along the coast for 50-100 THB per ride. Avoid taxi touts at the airport — book transfers in advance or use the Grab app.
content.nl: Huur een scooter (200-300 THB/dag) om de ringweg van Samui op eigen tempo te verkennen. Songthaews (gedeelde pick-up trucks) rijden langs de kust voor 50-100 THB per rit. Vermijd taxichauffeurs op het vliegveld — boek transfers van tevoren of gebruik de Grab app.

content.en: Chaweng is for action and nightlife, Lamai for a balance of buzz and relaxation, Bophut for culture and dining, Maenam for quiet seclusion, and Lipa Noi for the best sunsets. Match your beach to your vibe.
content.nl: Chaweng is voor actie en nachtleven, Lamai voor een balans van drukte en ontspanning, Bophut voor cultuur en uit eten, Maenam voor rustige afzondering en Lipa Noi voor de mooiste zonsondergangen. Kies je strand op basis van je stemming.

content.en: Book day trips to Ang Thong Marine Park (1,500-2,500 THB) or ferries to Koh Phangan (300-600 THB, 30 min) and Koh Tao (600-1,000 THB, 2 hours). Full Moon Party ferries to Phangan run monthly with late-night return boats.
content.nl: Boek dagtochten naar Ang Thong Marine Park (1.500-2.500 THB) of veerboten naar Koh Phangan (300-600 THB, 30 min) en Koh Tao (600-1.000 THB, 2 uur). Full Moon Party veerboten naar Phangan varen maandelijks met laat terugkeer boten.

content.en: Samui's Gulf coast means its rainy season (October-December) differs from the Andaman coast. Expect afternoon showers, not all-day rain. Prices drop significantly and beaches are less crowded. November can be very wet with occasional flooding.
content.nl: Samui's ligging aan de Golf betekent dat het regenseizoen (oktober-december) verschilt van de Andamankust. Verwacht middagbuien, geen hele dag regen. Prijzen dalen aanzienlijk en stranden zijn rustiger. November kan erg nat zijn met af en toe overstromingen.

content.en: Skip overpriced restaurants on Chaweng Beach Road and eat at Laem Din market stalls instead. Negotiate longtail boat prices before boarding. The 'elephant trekking' operations should be avoided — visit ethical sanctuaries instead.
content.nl: Sla te dure restaurants aan Chaweng Beach Road over en eet bij de marktkramen van Laem Din. Onderhandel over de prijzen van longtailboten voordat je instapt. De 'olifantentrekking' operaties kun je beter vermijden — bezoek in plaats daarvan ethische opvangcentra.

best_time_to_visit.en: December to April offers the best weather with sunny skies and calm seas, ideal for beach activities and island hopping. January to March is peak season with higher prices. The Gulf coast rainy season runs October to December, with November typically the wettest month.
best_time_to_visit.nl: December tot april biedt het beste weer met zonnige luchten en kalme zeeën, ideaal voor strandactiviteiten en eilandhoppen. Januari tot maart is het hoogseizoen met hogere prijzen. Het regenseizoen aan de Golfkust loopt van oktober tot december, met november als doorgaans de natste maand.

overview: Koh Samui, Thailand's second-largest island, is a tropical paradise located in the Gulf of Thailand within Surat Thani Province. With a population of around 63,000, this island has evolved into a sophisticated blend of natural beauty and luxury tourism, famous for its palm-fringed beaches, world-class resorts, and iconic landmarks like the Big Buddha Temple. From the vibrant 3km stretch of Chaweng Beach with its thriving nightlife to the serene Fisherman's Village in Bophut, Koh Samui offers diverse experiences for every type of traveler. The island serves as a gateway to the Ang Thong Marine Park, featuring 42 islands perfect for kayaking and snorkeling, while maintaining pristine natural attractions like Na Muang Waterfall and the unique Secret Buddha Garden.
thingsToDo: Koh Samui's attractions span cultural landmarks, natural wonders, and water activities. The iconic Big Buddha Temple (Wat Phra Yai) features a stunning 12-meter golden Buddha statue with panoramic sea views and an ornate Naga staircase—free to visit and best explored early morning. Chaweng Beach remains the island's most popular destination with 3km of white sand, offering water sports, beachside dining, and evening entertainment. For nature lovers, Ang Thong National Marine Park (300 THB/$9 entrance) showcases 42 islands with the famous emerald lake viewpoint and excellent kayaking opportunities. Cultural exploration continues at Wat Plai Laem with its distinctive 18-armed Guanyin statue set against a lakeside backdrop, while adventure seekers can hike to Na Muang Waterfall's natural swimming pools or visit the Samui Elephant Sanctuary (2,500 THB/$72) for ethical wildlife encounters. Don't miss Fisherman's Village Bophut's Friday Night Market featuring old Chinese shophouses and beachfront dining, or the hidden Secret Buddha Garden (80 THB/$2) accessible by 4WD through jungle terrain.
whereToStay: Koh Samui accommodates all budgets with diverse neighborhoods offering distinct experiences. The beachfront areas command premium prices—Chaweng offers proximity to nightlife and restaurants (2,500-6,000 THB/$75-180 per night for mid-range to boutique hotels), while quieter Lamai Beach provides relaxation near the distinctive Hin Ta Hin Yai rock formations. Bophut's Fisherman's Village blends cultural charm with upscale amenities, appealing to travelers seeking authentic experiences with modern comfort. Maenam Beach offers the island's most affordable options with traditional fishing village atmosphere and Koh Phangan views across the water. For long-term stays and digital nomads, monthly house rentals provide exceptional value: basic Thai houses start at 12,000-18,000 THB/month, mid-range modern homes rent for 20,000-30,000 THB/month with air-conditioning and Wi-Fi, and luxury pool villas reach 35,000-40,000+ THB/month. Hostels and budget guesthouses run 250-500 THB for shared beds and 600-1,200 THB for basic double rooms, making Samui accessible for backpackers while offering luxury villa experiences for those seeking indulgence.

whereToEat.name: Long Dtai
whereToEat.description: Located on secluded Cape Fahn private island accessible only by sea tractor or speedboat, Long Dtai features exceptional southern Thai cuisine curated by superstar chef David Thompson. The menu showcases local produce, sustainably sourced seafood, and line-caught fish with panoramic ocean vistas, including dishes like massaman curry and palm sugar dumplings in jasmine syrup.

whereToEat.name: The Rum Vault
whereToEat.description: Located at Four Seasons Resort, this intimate wood-panelled rum library offers an extensive collection of the world's finest spirits with master mixologists guiding connoisseurs through curated tastings and craft cocktails. It's a must-visit luxury experience inspired by The White Lotus series.

whereToEat.name: The Nature Samui
whereToEat.description: Hidden among verdant gardens and lotus ponds near the Samui Elephant Sanctuary, this farm-to-table retreat offers sustainable dining with opportunities to handpick organic ingredients and participate in interactive cooking classes. Their coconut milk and banana dessert is particularly noteworthy.

whereToEat.name: Chi Samui
whereToEat.description: Chi Samui channels Ibiza's sultry spirit with beachfront dining, fire dance shows, and sophisticated cocktails. This beach club combines stunning ocean views with upscale dining and entertainment in a vibrant atmosphere.

whereToEat.name: Ko Seng
whereToEat.description: A MICHELIN-listed Thai restaurant offering authentic local cuisine at accessible prices, making it a favorite among both tourists and locals seeking genuine flavors.

whereToEat.name: Kapi Sator
whereToEat.description: Located on Soi Had Chaweng 5, this MICHELIN-recognized restaurant serves Thai seafood specialties in a convenient beachfront location with authentic preparation methods.

whereToEat.name: Bang Por Seafood Takho
whereToEat.description: A MICHELIN-listed seafood restaurant offering fresh Thai-prepared catches with traditional recipes and genuine local flavors in a casual, relaxed setting.

whereToEat.name: Koh Thai Kitchen
whereToEat.description: A MICHELIN-recognized Thai kitchen serving authentic local dishes in a casual, accessible format perfect for experiencing genuine Thai cuisine without premium pricing.

topHotels.name: Four Seasons Resort Samui
topHotels.description: A prestigious resort discreetly set within a former coconut plantation featuring sprawling suites with private pools, jaw-dropping ocean vistas, and world-class dining including The Rum Vault rum library. Recently featured in HBO's The White Lotus season 3.

topHotels.name: Cape Fahn Hotel
topHotels.description: An exclusive beachfront property hosting the acclaimed Long Dtai restaurant and offering direct access to pristine private beaches with boutique accommodations and personalized service.

topHotels.name: Centara Grand Beach Resort Samui
topHotels.description: A well-established beachfront resort on Koh Samui's most popular beach offering comfortable rooms with sea views, multiple restaurants, and direct beach access with water sports facilities.

topHotels.name: Angsana Laguna Phuket Samui
topHotels.description: A resort-style property featuring lagoon-view rooms, family-friendly amenities, and convenient access to main attractions with reasonable rates for mid-range travelers.

topHotels.name: Bophut Beach Hotel
topHotels.description: A charming property in the Fisherman's Village area offering beachfront rooms, traditional Thai design elements, and proximity to Friday night markets and local dining.

topHotels.name: The Library
topHotels.description: A chic boutique resort known for modern minimalist design, stylish accommodations, excellent restaurants, and a vibrant beach club atmosphere appealing to sophisticated travelers.

topHotels.name: Samui First House
topHotels.description: An affordable guesthouse offering basic but clean rooms in central Chaweng with easy access to beaches, restaurants, and nightlife, ideal for budget travelers.

topHotels.name: P.S. Guesthouse
topHotels.description: A backpacker-friendly guesthouse in quieter Maenam Beach area providing dorm beds and simple private rooms with basic amenities at very affordable rates.

topHotels.name: Lamai Wanta
topHotels.description: A budget resort offering beachfront access on Lamai's quieter side with simple but adequate rooms, beach restaurant, and relaxed atmosphere perfect for budget-conscious travelers.

topHotels.name: Nora Beach Resort & Spa
topHotels.description: A secluded luxury resort on Koh Samui's quieter north coast featuring spa facilities, private beach access, romantic settings for couples, and personalized service in an intimate environment.

gettingAround: Koh Samui offers multiple transportation options for exploring the island's attractions. Tuk-tuks remain the most iconic choice for short distances within beaches and towns, though prices should be negotiated beforehand (typically 100-300 THB for short trips). Songthaews (shared red trucks) operate fixed routes between major areas like Chaweng, Lamai, and Bophut at 20-50 THB per person, making them ideal for budget travelers. For greater independence, motorbike rentals cost 150-300 THB daily and provide flexibility to explore beaches and inland attractions, though international driving permits are recommended. The mobile app Grab operates on Koh Samui offering metered motorcycle and car rides with transparent pricing, eliminating haggling and providing safety through GPS tracking. Renting a car or ATV ranges from 500-1,000 THB daily and suits travelers planning multi-beach itineraries, particularly for visiting the Secret Buddha Garden which requires 4WD access.
bestTimeToVisit: Koh Samui's ideal travel window is November through February, when dry weather, gentle breezes, and calm seas create perfect conditions for beach activities and island-hopping excursions to Ang Thong Marine Park. December and January represent peak season with abundant sunshine, temperatures around 28-30°C (82-86°F), and minimal rainfall, though accommodations book quickly and prices increase. February extends this excellent weather window with slightly fewer crowds while maintaining dry conditions. March through May brings hot, humid conditions with temperatures exceeding 33°C (91°F) and occasional afternoon showers, making beach days less comfortable but reducing tourist numbers significantly. The southwest monsoon season from May through October brings heavy rainfall, choppy seas, and increased humidity; while some rain-resistant travelers visit during these months for lower prices and fewer crowds, water sports become risky and visibility for snorkeling diminishes considerably. Avoid September and October entirely when rainfall peaks and typhoon risks increase, making island visits treacherous.

budget.description: Budget travelers can subsist on 20-40 USD daily: sleep in hostels or basic guesthouses (250-500 THB/$7-15), eat street food and local restaurants (50-100 THB per meal/$1.50-3), use songthaews and tuk-tuks for transport (50-100 THB/$1.50-3 daily), and visit free attractions like beaches and Big Buddha Temple. This allows occasional paid activities like Ang Thong Marine Park.

midrange.description: Mid-range travelers spending 50-100 USD daily can stay in 3-star hotels (1,500-3,000 THB/$45-90), dine at quality restaurants (200-400 THB per meal/$6-12), enjoy organized tours and activities (800-2,000 THB/$24-60), and use convenient transport like Grab rides. This budget accommodates comfort without luxury indulgences.

luxury.description: Luxury travelers spending 150-400 USD daily stay in 5-star resorts (4,000-15,000+ THB/$120-450), dine at fine restaurants and beachclubs (1,000-3,000 THB per meal/$30-90), book private tours and water activities (3,000-8,000 THB/$90-240), and enjoy spa treatments and exclusive experiences. This includes premium accommodations at Four Seasons and similar properties.

safetyTips: Koh Samui is generally safe for tourists, though standard precautions apply: avoid walking alone late night in Chaweng's nightlife areas where theft and drink-spiking occasionally occur, keep valuables secure in hotel safes rather than on beaches, and be cautious with motorbike rentals—wear helmets (legally required), drive defensively on narrow island roads, and avoid riding under influence. Water safety requires attention during monsoon months (May-October) when strong currents and rough seas make swimming dangerous, particularly on exposed beaches. Use designated swimming areas at busy beaches where lifeguards operate. Petty theft occurs in crowded areas and beaches; secure bags while swimming and avoid displaying expensive jewelry, cameras, or large cash amounts. Traffic accidents represent the primary danger; Thai drivers may ignore traffic rules, so exercise extreme caution when crossing roads or riding motorbikes. Respect local customs by avoiding disrespect to the Thai royal family and being mindful at temples—dress modestly and remove shoes when entering religious spaces.

faq.question: What is the best time to visit Koh Samui?
faq.answer: November through February offers the best weather with dry conditions, calm seas, and temperatures around 28-30°C (82-86°F). December and January are peak season with highest prices, while February provides similar weather with fewer crowds. Avoid September and October during peak monsoon season when heavy rainfall and typhoon risks make island activities treacherous.

faq.question: How much does it cost to visit Koh Samui per day?
faq.answer: Budget travelers can visit on 20-40 USD daily with hostel stays and street food. Mid-range travelers spend 50-100 USD daily staying in 3-star hotels and mid-range restaurants. Luxury travelers should budget 150-400 USD daily for 5-star resorts and fine dining experiences. Paid attractions like Ang Thong Marine Park cost 300 THB ($9) and the Samui Elephant Sanctuary costs 2,500 THB ($72).

faq.question: How do I get around Koh Samui?
faq.answer: Tuk-tuks (100-300 THB for short trips), songthaews/shared trucks (20-50 THB per person), and Grab app rides provide convenient transport. Motorbike rentals (150-300 THB daily) offer independence, while car/ATV rentals (500-1,000 THB daily) suit multi-destination exploration. All methods are widely available and affordable for tourists.

faq.question: Is Koh Samui safe for tourists?
faq.answer: Yes, Koh Samui is generally safe. Standard precautions include avoiding late-night walks in Chaweng's nightlife areas, securing valuables in hotel safes, wearing helmets on motorbikes, and exercising caution crossing roads. Petty theft occurs in crowded areas, so avoid displaying expensive items. Water safety requires attention during monsoon months (May-October) when currents are dangerous.

faq.question: What are the main attractions in Koh Samui?
faq.answer: Top attractions include the Big Buddha Temple (Wat Phra Yai) with free entry and panoramic views, Chaweng Beach (3km of white sand), Ang Thong Marine Park (300 THB/$9 with 42 islands and kayaking), Fisherman's Village Bophut with Friday night markets, Lamai Beach with Hin Ta Hin Yai rock formations, Na Muang Waterfall, and the Samui Elephant Sanctuary (2,500 THB/$72).

faq.question: What is the average hotel cost in Koh Samui?
faq.answer: Budget guesthouses cost 600-1,200 THB ($18-36) per night. Mid-range 3-star hotels range from 1,500-3,000 THB ($45-90) daily. Luxury beachfront resorts and boutique hotels cost 2,500-6,000+ THB ($75-180) nightly. For long-term stays, house rentals provide better value at 12,000-40,000 THB ($360-1,200) monthly depending on location and amenities.

faq.question: Do I need a visa to visit Koh Samui?
faq.answer: Visa requirements depend on your nationality. Most Western tourists receive 30-day visa exemptions upon arrival at Thai immigration. Check with your country's Thai embassy for specific requirements. The nearest airport is Samui International Airport (USM), approximately 20-30 minutes by car from major beaches, with flight connections to Bangkok and other Thai destinations.

practicalInfo.travelTimeFromAirport: 20-30 minutes by car/tuk-tuk to major beaches
